edges.description: Mediator dysfunction perturbs neuronal developmental gene-expression programs that contribute to developmental delay, language impairment, and intellectual disability.
Mediator dysfunction can also perturb cardiac developmental transcriptional programs, contributing to variable congenital heart anomalies in a subset of patients.
Impaired SCF-Fbw7-mediated MED13 turnover can alter CDK8-module availability at the Mediator complex and intensify transcriptional dysregulation.
edges.source: MED13 phosphodegron disruption and impaired protein turnover

edges.description: Disrupted neuronal developmental programs contribute to the core cognitive impairment phenotype.
Early developmental milestone acquisition is impaired downstream of neuronal transcriptional dysregulation.
Language acquisition is especially sensitive to the disrupted neurodevelopmental program in MED13-related disorder.
Variable downstream neurobehavioral manifestations can include autistic features in a subset of affected individuals.
Variable downstream neurobehavioral manifestations can include ADHD in a subset of affected individuals.
Perturbed cardiac developmental programs contribute to the variable congenital heart phenotype reported in a subset of MED13 cases.

nodes.description: ADHD has been described in some individuals with MED13 mutations.

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in MED13, a component of the Mediator complex, are associated with a novel neurodevelopmental disorder.
evidence.snippet: Other features that were reported in two or more patients include autism spectrum disorder, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, optic nerve abnormalities, Duane anomaly, hypotonia, mild congenital heart abnormalities, and dysmorphisms.

nodes.description: Autism spectrum disorder has been reported in a subset of individuals with MED13-related disorder. The syndrome-defining cohort supports ASD as a recurrent but non-universal feature. A 2024 single-case report described ASD in a child with an uncertain heterozygous MED13 variant and should be interpreted cautiously.

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in MED13, a component of the Mediator complex, are associated with a novel neurodevelopmental disorder.
evidence.snippet: Other features that were reported in two or more patients include autism spectrum disorder, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, optic nerve abnormalities, Duane anomaly, hypotonia, mild congenital heart abnormalities, and dysmorphisms.

evidence.reference_title: MED13 Gene Mutation Related to Autism Spectrum Disorder: A Case Report.
evidence.snippet: In this case report, we present a case of a child diagnosed with ASD who underwent whole exome sequencing (WES) and revealed an uncertain heterozygous variant in the MED13 gene.

nodes.description: In a subset of affected individuals, MED13/Mediator dysfunction perturbs cardiac developmental gene-expression programs, contributing to mild and variable congenital heart anomalies.
Mild congenital heart abnormalities have been reported in a subset of patients. The cardiac phenotype is variable and typically mild.

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in MED13, a component of the Mediator complex, are associated with a novel neurodevelopmental disorder.
evidence.snippet: Other features that were reported in two or more patients include autism spectrum disorder, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, optic nerve abnormalities, Duane anomaly, hypotonia, mild congenital heart abnormalities, and dysmorphisms.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding phenotype of MED13-associated syndrome presenting novel de novo missense variant in a patient with multiple congenital anomalies.
evidence.snippet: He presented with congenital heart anomalies, dysmorphic features, hydrocephalic changes, hypoplastic corpus callosum, bilateral optic nerve atrophy, optic chiasm atrophy, brain stem atrophy, and overall a more severe condition compared to previously described patients.

nodes.description: Speech and language delay is highly prevalent and often the most prominent developmental concern. Language impairment may be more severely affected than motor development.

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in MED13, a component of the Mediator complex, are associated with a novel neurodevelopmental disorder.
evidence.snippet: All patients had intellectual disability and/or developmental delays, including speech delays or disorders.

evidence.reference_title: A novel frameshift variant in the MED13 gene causing intellectual developmental disorder-61 in a Chinese family.
evidence.snippet: The main symptoms are intellectual disability (ID) of varying degrees, developmental delay (DD), hypotonia during infancy, facial dysmorphism, language impairment, restricted growth, skeletal and limb abnormalities, and behavioral abnormalities.

nodes.description: MED13 is a subunit of the CDK8 kinase module of the Mediator complex, which regulates RNA polymerase II-dependent transcription. MED13 plays a connective role between the CDK8 kinase module and the Mediator core. Pathogenic variants disrupt Mediator-dependent transcriptional control and broader gene-expression programs. MED13 belongs to the group of CDK8-kinase module-associated disease genes, alongside MED12, MED13L, and CDK8.

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in MED13, a component of the Mediator complex, are associated with a novel neurodevelopmental disorder.
evidence.snippet: MED13 is a component of the CDK8-kinase module that can reversibly bind Mediator, a multi-protein complex that is required for Polymerase II transcription initiation. Mutations in several other genes encoding subunits of Mediator have been previously shown to associate with DD/ID, including MED13L, a paralog of MED13. Thus, our findings add MED13 to the group of CDK8-kinase module-associated disease genes.

evidence.reference_title: Potential roles of mediator Complex Subunit 13 in Cardiac Diseases.
evidence.snippet: MED13 plays a connective role between the CKM and the MED core

nodes.description: Global developmental delay with delayed motor and cognitive milestones is present in most affected children. Severity is variable.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding phenotype of MED13-associated syndrome presenting novel de novo missense variant in a patient with multiple congenital anomalies.
evidence.snippet: de novo variants in the MED13 gene were described in patients with an intellectual developmental disorder that included global developmental delay, mild congenital heart anomalies, and hearing and vision problems in some patients.

evidence.reference_title: Intellectual Developmental Disorder of Autosomal Dominant 61 Caused by a MED13 Variant Presenting With Congenital Unilateral Sensorineural Hearing Loss: A Case Report.
evidence.snippet: We report the case of an eight-year-old male with global developmental delay (GDD), an intellectual developmental disorder (IDD), mild dysmorphic features, congenital unilateral sensorineural hearing loss (SNHL), and a supernumerary left maxillary central incisor.

nodes.description: Intellectual disability ranging from mild to severe is a core feature, present in all or nearly all affected individuals. The degree of impairment varies considerably between patients.

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in MED13, a component of the Mediator complex, are associated with a novel neurodevelopmental disorder.
evidence.snippet: All patients had intellectual disability and/or developmental delays, including speech delays or disorders.

evidence.reference_title: A novel frameshift variant in the MED13 gene causing intellectual developmental disorder-61 in a Chinese family.
evidence.snippet: The main symptoms are intellectual disability (ID) of varying degrees, developmental delay (DD), hypotonia during infancy, facial dysmorphism, language impairment, restricted growth, skeletal and limb abnormalities, and behavioral abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in MED13, a component of the Mediator complex, are associated with a novel neurodevelopmental disorder.
evidence.snippet: Here we describe a GeneMatcher collaboration which led to a cohort of 13 affected individuals harboring protein-altering variants, 11 of which are de novo, in MED13; the only inherited variant was transmitted to an affected child from an affected mother.

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in MED13, a component of the Mediator complex, are associated with a novel neurodevelopmental disorder.
evidence.snippet: Six affected individuals had mutations that are predicted to truncate the MED13 protein, six had missense mutations, and one had an in-frame-deletion of one amino acid. Out of the seven non-truncating mutations, six clustered in two specific locations of the MED13 protein: an N-terminal and C-terminal region.

evidence.reference_title: A novel frameshift variant in the MED13 gene causing intellectual developmental disorder-61 in a Chinese family.
evidence.snippet: Intellectual developmental disorder type 61 (MRD61) is an extremely rare autosomal dominant disorder caused by variants in the MED13 gene. This gene encodes a subunit of the mediator complex, which is also known as TRAP, SMCC, DRIP or ARC. This complex functions as a transcriptional coactivator and is essential for the expression of almost all genes. To date, only 26 cases of MRD61 have been reported worldwide.

evidence.reference_title: Intellectual Developmental Disorder of Autosomal Dominant 61 Caused by a MED13 Variant Presenting With Congenital Unilateral Sensorineural Hearing Loss: A Case Report.
evidence.snippet: This case is also consistent with haploinsufficiency as the disease mechanism for truncating MED13 variants in MRD61, underscoring the importance of exome sequencing in patients with neurodevelopmental disorders and congenital anomalies.

nodes.description: Several pathogenic missense variants cluster at a conserved phosphodegron (Thr326/Pro327) in MED13. This phosphodegron is normally recognized by the SCF-Fbw7 ubiquitin ligase complex, leading to MED13 ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation. Mutations at these residues may impair MED13 protein turnover, potentially resulting in gain-of-function or dominant-negative effects through accumulation of mutant protein.
nodes.id: MED13 phosphodegron disruption and impaired protein turnover

biological_processes: SCF-dependent proteasomal ubiquitin-dependent protein catabolic process

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in MED13, a component of the Mediator complex, are associated with a novel neurodevelopmental disorder.
evidence.snippet: The four N-terminal clustering mutations affect two adjacent amino acids that are known to be involved in MED13 ubiquitination and degradation, p.Thr326 and p.Pro327.

evidence.reference_title: Potential roles of mediator Complex Subunit 13 in Cardiac Diseases.
evidence.snippet: Normally, this phosphodegron is recognized by the S-phase kinase-associated protein (Skp)-Cullin-F-box (SCF) F-box and WD repeat domain-containing 7 (Fbw7) ubiquitin ligase complex (SCFFbw7), resulting in MED13 protein ubiquitination and degradation

nodes.description: Downstream of MED13/Mediator dysfunction, neuronal gene-expression programs are dysregulated during brain development, contributing to intellectual disability, global developmental delay, language impairment, and variable behavioral phenotypes.
